title: DevOps Portfolio Website
shortDescription: Full-stack cloud resume website with a React frontend hosted on Azure Blob Storage, a serverless visitor counter API, and CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions.
detailedDescription: Built a cloud-native portfolio website. The frontend is a React + TypeScript application styled with Tailwind CSS and bundled with Vite, hosted as a static website on Azure Blob Storage behind Cloudflare CDN. The backend is a serverless Azure Function (Node.js) that tracks visitor counts in Azure Cosmos DB. Both frontend and backend are automatically built and deployed via GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines on every push to main.
documentation: The frontend uses React Router with HashRouter for client-side navigation across Home, Projects, Resume, and Project Detail pages. The visitor counter API validates CORS origins, increments a counter document in Cosmos DB, and returns the updated count. The frontend workflow builds with Vite, uploads the dist/ folder to the $web container in Azure Storage, and optionally purges the Cloudflare cache. The backend workflow deploys the Azure Function and verifies the deployment with a health check.

title: CI/CD Pipeline for Containerized Apps on Amazon EKS
shortDescription: End-to-end GitOps pipeline that builds, scans, and deploys containerized applications to Amazon EKS using GitLab CI/CD, Amazon ECR, and ArgoCD Image Updater.
detailedDescription: Built an automated CI/CD pipeline where every push to main triggers a full workflow: Docker image build, Hadolint linting, Snyk security scanning, and push to Amazon ECR with a Git SHA-based tag. ArgoCD Image Updater polls ECR for new tags, commits the updated image reference back to the Helm chart in Git, and ArgoCD syncs the change to the EKS cluster — achieving fully automated, zero-touch GitOps delivery with rolling updates.
documentation: The pipeline runs five stages: platform_check (AWS CLI and kubectl connectivity verification), validate (Dockerfile linting with Hadolint), build_and_package (Docker build and ECR push tagged with CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA), scan (Snyk container vulnerability scanning), and deploy_dev (optional ArgoCD sync trigger). ArgoCD Image Updater uses a newest-build strategy filtered by a SHA regex to detect new images, then performs a Git write-back to update the Helm values.yaml. IRSA provides ECR read access to Image Updater, and a GitLab Project Access Token enables the Git write-back. The application is packaged as a Helm chart under charts/myapp with configurable image repository, tag, and resource settings.

title: CI/CD Pipeline with GitHub Actions
shortDescription: End-to-end CI/CD pipeline automating build, test, security scanning, and deployment across multiple environments.
detailedDescription: Designed a comprehensive CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions with multi-stage workflows. Includes automated unit testing, integration testing, container image building with Docker, security scanning with Trivy and Snyk, infrastructure validation with Terraform plan, and blue-green deployments to Azure. Implements branch protection, required reviews, and automated rollback capabilities.
documentation: The pipeline follows trunk-based development. Feature branches trigger PR builds with automated testing. Merges to main trigger staging deployments. Production releases are triggered by tags and include canary analysis before full rollout.

title: Infrastructure as Code with Terraform
shortDescription: Complete Azure infrastructure managed with Terraform modules, remote state, and automated drift detection.
detailedDescription: Developed reusable Terraform modules for provisioning Azure infrastructure including Virtual Networks, AKS clusters, Azure SQL databases, Key Vaults, and Application Gateways. Implemented remote state management with Azure Storage backend, state locking, and workspace-based environment separation. Automated drift detection runs daily via scheduled pipelines.
documentation: Infrastructure is organized into layered modules: networking, compute, data, and security. Each module is versioned independently and published to a private Terraform registry. Changes are applied through a PR-based workflow with mandatory plan review.

title: Observability Stack with Prometheus & Grafana
shortDescription: Full observability platform with metrics, logging, tracing, and alerting for microservices architecture.
detailedDescription: Deployed a comprehensive observability stack using Prometheus for metrics collection, Grafana for visualization, Loki for log aggregation, and Jaeger for distributed tracing. Implemented custom Prometheus exporters for business metrics, created Grafana dashboards for SLI/SLO tracking, and configured multi-channel alerting through Alertmanager with PagerDuty and Slack integrations.
documentation: The observability stack follows the three pillars approach: metrics (Prometheus), logs (Loki), and traces (Jaeger). All components are deployed on Kubernetes using Helm charts. Data retention policies and storage optimization ensure cost-effective long-term storage.

title: Azure Cloud Infrastructure Deployment
shortDescription: Enterprise-grade Azure landing zone with hub-spoke network topology, security controls, and governance policies.
detailedDescription: Architected and deployed an Azure landing zone following the Cloud Adoption Framework. Implemented hub-spoke network topology with Azure Firewall, configured Azure Policy for governance and compliance, set up Azure Monitor and Log Analytics for centralized monitoring, and deployed Azure Key Vault for secrets management. Includes disaster recovery with Azure Site Recovery and backup policies.
documentation: The landing zone follows Microsoft's Cloud Adoption Framework with customizations for the organization's compliance requirements. Network segmentation uses NSGs and Azure Firewall rules. Identity management is centralized through Azure AD with Conditional Access policies.

title: Container Security Pipeline
shortDescription: Automated container security scanning and policy enforcement across the container lifecycle.
detailedDescription: Built an automated container security pipeline that scans images at build time, enforces admission policies at deploy time, and monitors for vulnerabilities at runtime. Integrates Trivy for image scanning, OPA Gatekeeper for Kubernetes admission control, and Falco for runtime security monitoring. Includes automated remediation workflows for critical vulnerabilities.
documentation: Security is enforced at every stage: Dockerfiles are linted with hadolint, images are scanned with Trivy, Kubernetes manifests are validated with OPA policies, and runtime behavior is monitored with Falco. Findings are aggregated in a security dashboard.
